column2: Key chemical equation evaluated in this step.
column5: Li + organic extractant; strip to aqueous
column6: Li+ + (CO3)2- --> Li2CO3 via addition of Na2CO3. Assume output is 20% Li2CO3. Conversion factor of L_brine to L_slurry: calculated via determined by density_slurry mg/L * (mg Li2CO3 / mg slurry) * (mg Li / mg Li2CO3) where density_slurry = wt slurry/ L_slurry = 1.118 g/cm^3 = 1.118 mg/L (see step 5 notes). wt Li2CO3 / wt slurry = 20% from assumption. wt Li / wt Li2CO3 = 2*6.94/73.89 = 0.188 --> 1.118*0.20*0.188 mg Li / L slurry. Multiply by 10^3 to get mg Li / m3 slurry. This would be multiplied by the L_slurry and divided by Li concentration from input brine concentration (* 1 / (mg Li / L brine))) to get the L of brine.
column7: Pressing for higher density. 20% Li2CO3 --> 70% Li2CO3, using equation defined in step 7 (also at end of this note), takes 1.118 g/cm^3 slurry to 1.58 g/cm^3 cake. We go from ~223.6 kg Li2CO3 and 894.4 kg water in the slurry per m^3 to 223.6 kg Li2CO3 and 95.8 kg water in the 70/30 cake. 319.4 kg / 1.58 kg/m^3 ≈ 0.202 m^3 cake per 1 m^3 slurry. equation: density = 1 / (r_Li2CO3 / rho_Li2CO3 + (1 - r_Li2CO3) / (rho_water))
column8: Removal of any remaining soluble Na/K/Mg from cake via water washing
column9: Based on density (function of constituent fractions); mass_Li2CO3 (V_cake) = V_cake / (r_Li2CO3 / rho_Li2CO3 + (1 - r_Li2CO3) / (rho_water)) * yield, where r_Li2CO3 is the ratio of Li2CO3 to total cake mass, in this case assumed to be 70%. With rho_Li2CO3 = 2.11 g/cm3 and assuming relatively pure water (rho_water ≈ 1 g/cm3), we need roughly 0.903 L of cake per kg Li2CO3. Since the conversion is from m3 of cake to tonnes Li2CO3, divide 1 (m3-->L but kg-->tonnes) by 0.903

column3: Mg removal via Ca(OH)2 at pH ~10.8–11.2; 15% excess over stoichiometric ratio of Ca(OH)2 to Mg2+ ions; lime slurry ~10 wt %; clarifier SOR ~33–49 m3/m2/d; mixing sized on P/V; Ca removal deferred to soda ash step.
column4: Boron extraction with resin or solvent; typical removal 90–95%
column5: Multi-stage extraction of Li+ into organic; scrub to reduce co-extracted Na/K; strip to produce a LiCl aqueous concentrate; typical 2–4 extraction stages + 1 scrub + 1–2 strip stages; organic = extractant + modifier + diluent; Li recovery per pass 80–95%; eluate sent to carbonate precipitation.
column6: Li precipitation as carbonate at pH ~10.5; 5% Na2CO3 excess.
column8: Wash to remove residual Na, K, Mg
column9: Drying to ~99% Li₂CO₃, bagging for sale
